subject: Chemistry
title: The Chemistry of Fire
description: A quick win: understand The Chemistry of Fire.

hook.text: Fire isn't a thing—it's a process. It's the visible result of a rapid chemical reaction called combustion.

buildup.text: Combustion needs three things: fuel, oxygen, and heat. Remove any one and the fire dies.

discovery.text: When fuel burns, it reacts with oxygen to produce carbon dioxide, water, heat, and light.

twist.text: Humans controlled fire over a million years ago. But we didn't understand the chemistry until the 1770s.

climax.text: Lavoisier proved combustion was a reaction with oxygen, killing the old 'phlogiston' theory.

punchline.text: Fire isn't magic. It's oxygen grabbing electrons from fuel.

quiz.question: What three things does combustion require?
